designPrinciples: Separate 'what is it' (ingredient concept) from 'how is it bought' (pack/SKU contract)
Never chain price_history across non-equivalent pack contracts
Persist identity decisions — do not rely solely on in-memory token scoring
Supplier intelligence compares within equivalence class only

optionsEvaluated.description: Keep ingredients as concept; add ingredient_pack_variants child table with own current_price, purchase_quantity, base_unit
pros: Minimal disruption to recipe_ingredients (bind to concept or preferred variant)
Natural fit for Mozzarella piece vs 2kg block
cons: Recipes must pick variant or use 'preferred/default variant'
Migration of existing history rows requires variant inference
optionsEvaluated.vlFit: Excellent — directly fixes Mozzarella and Pepino fresco/conserva split

optionsEvaluated.description: New products/skus table; ingredients become abstract concepts; invoices match to SKU
pros: Supports same SKU from multiple suppliers

optionsEvaluated.description: equivalence_groups table; members share recipe substitution; each member has explicit base_unit contract
pros: Enables 'use any of these packs in recipe' without false price chaining
cons: Complex UX for confirming group membership

optionsEvaluated.description: Extend ingredient_aliases with pack_variant_id, purchase_quantity snapshot, confirmed_format_hash
cons: Does not fix history chaining alone
optionsEvaluated.vlFit: Partial — helps matching, not pricing ledger

optionsEvaluated.description: Guard in appendIngredientPriceHistoryFromInvoiceLine: skip delta if purchase_quantity/base_unit contract differs

phase0_immediate.name: Cross-format history guard (Option E)
actions: Persist purchase_quantity + cost_base_unit on ingredient_price_history rows
Block storedPrev chain when contract hash differs from new row
Flag opportunities as 'format_change' not 'price_spike' when contract differs

phase1_foundation.name: Pack variants under ingredient concept (Option A + D)
phase1_foundation.matching: Resolve invoice line → pack_variant_id (or suggest new variant under concept)

phase2_intelligence.name: Equivalence groups for supplier intelligence (Option C)
actions: Supplier comparisons only within group + same base_unit contract
Opportunity: switch supplier only when variants are marked equivalent

phase3_optional.name: SKU layer (Option B)
phase3_optional.when: Multi-site, distributor integrations, or barcode scanning

migrationNotes.mozzarella: Split 2a99cecd into variants: piece-tray (pq=10, €0.95/op) and block-2kg (pq=1, €13.69)
migrationNotes.pepino: Split 635a1189: conserva jar (€/kg-op ~3.75) vs fresco kg (€/g-op 0.00177) — or separate ingredient concepts
migrationNotes.gingerBeer: Variant per volume SKU (20cl, 33cl); volume parse fix still required
migrationNotes.existingHistory: Backfill pack_variant_id via invoice line replay; mark ambiguous rows non-chainable

finalQuestion.question: Is Ingredient Identity now the highest-leverage Marginly foundation problem remaining?

ingredientIdentity.reason: Proven VL failures (+1341%, -99.95%) poison opportunities, supplier intel, and recipe costing under one ID; no schema dimension for pack format

staleDbReread.reason: Refreshes invoice_items and history from v31 — does not fix cross-format ID collapse

gingerBeerVolumeParse.reason: Real math bug but affects beverage SKUs with ambiguous CL tokens; orthogonal to ID schema

gtCatalogPomodor.reason: Harness/validation issue; extraction matches visible invoice

gptVarianceTails.reason: v31 closed structural families; Farina 5% / Gorgonzola intermittent — prompt ROI low per closure audit

finalQuestion.recommendation: Treat Ingredient Identity (pack-variant layer + history chain guard) as the highest-leverage **foundation** work before scaling opportunities/supplier intelligence. Execute stale VL re-read in parallel as a quick operational win — it unblocks data freshness but does not replace identity architecture.
priorityOrder: 1. Cross-format history guard (Option E) — stop false opportunity signals
2. Pack variant schema (Option A) — foundation for trustworthy intelligence
4. Ginger Beer volume parse guard — beverage edge case
